title: plagiocheilus soliviformis subsp. soliviformis
intro: Plagiocheilus soliviformis subsp. soliviformis, also known as the "Himalayan earthwormwort" or "Indian earthwormwort", is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Plagiocheilus soliviformis subsp. soliviformis include treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin problems, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used in traditional medicine to make teas, infusions, and poultices, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy.
